conversation.message: How have you been feeling lately, overall?
I’m okay, I guess. Just a bit tired. I haven’t been sleeping well, and I think it’s catching up with me.
How long has the tiredness been affecting your day?
About a week, maybe two? I get through the morning, but by lunchtime, I just want to go back to bed.
Is it harder to focus or concentrate than it used to be?
Yeah. I sit down to do my work, and my mind just starts to wander. It’s like I’m reading a sentence, but I have no idea what it says.
How long does it usually take before your mind starts to wander like that?
Usually just a few minutes. It’s like my brain just hits a wall and stops.
How have you been sleeping lately?
Not great. I wake up a lot, usually around 2 or 3 am, and then I just lie there. It’s like I’m waiting for something to happen, but I don't know what.
When you're lying awake at that hour, what kinds of thoughts or feelings come up?
Just worries, mostly. I worry I'm not doing enough, or that I’m wasting my time. It’s stupid, I know, but it’s hard to quiet my brain sometimes.
Been doing anything you enjoy lately?
Not really. I haven't had the energy for much. I usually just sit and scroll through my phone until I fall asleep.
How long has it felt like too much effort to do the things you usually enjoy?
A few months, maybe? It’s like I used to love reading or watching movies, but now it just feels like a chore.
When you think about the next few weeks, what do you expect day to day?
I don't really know. I just try to get through the next day and then worry about the next one later.
Does it feel like each day is something you have to push through?
Yeah. It feels like I’m just going through the motions, waiting for something to change or for my energy to come back.
Do you find it harder to make decisions than before, even small ones?
Yeah, a lot. I just want to pick the easiest option usually. I don't have the energy to weigh all the possibilities.
How long has it felt like too much effort to weigh your options?
About the same amount of time, I think. It’s like I used to be able to decide what to wear in the morning, and now it takes me 20 minutes just to pick a shirt.
Do you feel like you have enough energy for your usual day?
No, definitely not. I have to pace myself just to get through the day.
What does pacing yourself look like on a typical day?
I take a lot of breaks. I sit down for 15 minutes, then get up and do something else. It helps, but it feels like I’m just putting off the inevitable crash later.
Are you still into the things you used to care about?
A little, but not as much. I used to love my history course, but now it just feels like a chore.
What's something else that used to bring you joy but now feels like a chore?
Probably just seeing my friends. I love them, but I just can't keep up the conversation anymore.
Is there anything weighing on your mind that you wish you could change?
Yeah. I just wish I could be more normal, you know? Like I’m not tired all the time.
What does being "normal" look like to you?
Just being able to get through the day without feeling like I’m running on empty.
How do you feel about how things have gone for you recently?
I guess I’m just... frustrated. I want to be able to do more, but I can’t even make it through a day without wanting to go to bed.
What's stopping you from doing the things you want to do?
My energy, mostly. I just get tired, and then I’m useless for the rest of the day.
How's your appetite been?
It’s been okay, I guess. I eat when I can, but I’m not really hungry most of the time.
Have you noticed any changes in your weight because of that?
A bit, maybe. I’ve lost a few pounds, but I don't really care.
Have you found yourself getting emotional more easily lately?
Yeah, sorry. I get a little overwhelmed sometimes, and then I just... snap.
